name: supabase
context: Supabase platform — Postgres database, auth, storage, realtime, edge functions, and vector embeddings
info: The Supabase ecosystem covers the open-source Firebase alternative built on Postgres: authentication with 20+ OAuth providers and magic links, a full Postgres database exposed via auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs (PostgREST), Row Level Security for fine-grained access control, S3-compatible file storage with image transformations, realtime subscriptions via WebSocket channels (Postgres changes, broadcast, presence), Deno-based edge functions for server-side logic, and pgvector for AI embedding storage and similarity search. The @supabase/supabase-js SDK is the primary client. The Supabase CLI handles local development, migrations, and deployment. All services share a single Postgres instance as the source of truth — auth tables, storage metadata, and realtime config all live in Postgres schemas.
body:
# Supabase Ecosystem

Supabase is an open-source backend-as-a-service built on Postgres. Every Supabase project is a full Postgres database with auto-generated APIs, authentication, file storage, realtime subscriptions, edge functions, and vector search — all managed through a unified dashboard, CLI, and client SDK.

## Architecture

Every Supabase project runs these services around a single Postgres instance:

| Service | Component | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| Database | Postgres 15+ | Primary data store, source of truth for everything |
| API | PostgREST | Auto-generated REST API from your Postgres schema |
| Auth | GoTrue | User authentication and JWT issuance |
| Storage | S3-compatible | File uploads with policies and image transformations |
| Realtime | Elixir server | WebSocket subscriptions for Postgres changes, broadcast, presence |
| Edge Functions | Deno Deploy | Server-side TypeScript/JavaScript functions |
| Vectors | pgvector | Embedding storage and similarity search |
| CLI | [[supabase/cli]] | Local dev, migrations, deployment, type generation |
| Client | [[supabase/client]] | @supabase/supabase-js SDK for all services |

## Agent Jobs

- **Initialize a project** — Use [[supabase/cli]] to run `supabase init` and `supabase start` for local development
- **Authenticate users** — Implement sign-up, sign-in, OAuth, and magic links with [[supabase/auth]]
- **Query data** — Select, insert, update, delete with filters via [[supabase/database]]
- **Secure data** — Write Row Level Security policies with [[supabase/rls]]
- **Manage schema** — Create and run migrations with [[supabase/migrations]]
- **Upload files** — Manage buckets and upload files with [[supabase/storage]]
- **Subscribe to changes** — Listen for database changes, broadcast messages, track presence via [[supabase/realtime]]
- **Run server code** — Deploy Deno edge functions with [[supabase/edge-functions]]
- **Store embeddings** — Use pgvector for AI similarity search via [[supabase/vectors]]
- **Use the SDK** — Initialize and configure @supabase/supabase-js via [[supabase/client]]

## Environment Variables

Every Supabase integration requires these variables:

| Variable | Required | Purpose |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `SUPABASE_URL` | Yes | Project API URL (`https://<ref>.supabase.co`) |
| `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Yes | Public anon key for client-side access (safe to expose, guarded by RLS) |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Server only | Bypasses RLS — never expose to clients |
| `SUPABASE_DB_URL` | Optional | Direct Postgres connection string for migrations/ORMs |
| `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` | CLI only | Personal access token for CLI and Management API |

For Next.js and other frameworks, prefix with `NEXT_PUBLIC_`:
```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://abcdefgh.supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...
```

## Client Initialization

```typescript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';

const supabase = createClient(
  process.env.SUPABASE_URL!,
  process.env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
);
```

## Project Structure

A typical Supabase project directory:

```
my-app/
├── supabase/
│   ├── config.toml          # Local dev configuration
│   ├── migrations/           # SQL migration files
│   │   └── 20240101000000_init.sql
│   ├── functions/            # Edge functions
│   │   └── hello/
│   │       └── index.ts
│   └── seed.sql              # Seed data
├── src/
│   └── lib/
│       └── supabase.ts       # Client initialization
├── .env.local                # Environment variables
└── package.json
```

## Key Concepts

- **Anon key vs Service role key**: The anon key is safe for client-side code because Row Level Security controls access. The service role key bypasses all RLS and must only be used server-side.
- **PostgREST**: Supabase auto-generates a REST API from your Postgres schema. Every table, view, and function becomes an API endpoint. No code generation needed.
- **Postgres is the platform**: Auth users live in `auth.users`, storage metadata in `storage.objects`, realtime config in `realtime.*`. You can join across all of them with standard SQL.

sections.title: Platform architecture
sections.body: Supabase wraps a standard Postgres 15+ instance with GoTrue (auth), PostgREST (REST API), Realtime (Elixir WebSocket server), Storage (S3-compatible), and Deno-based edge functions. All services share the same Postgres database. Auth tables live in `auth.*`, storage metadata in `storage.*`. The client SDK @supabase/supabase-js provides a unified interface to all services. Local development uses Docker containers via `supabase start`.

sections.title: Environment variables
sections.body: `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are required for all client-side usage. The anon key is safe to expose because [[supabase/rls]] controls data access. `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` bypasses RLS and must only be used server-side (edge functions, API routes, cron jobs). `SUPABASE_DB_URL` provides a direct Postgres connection for ORMs like [[prisma]] or Drizzle. `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` authenticates the CLI and Management API.

sections.title: Local development
sections.body: Run `supabase init` to scaffold a project, then `supabase start` to launch local Docker containers (Postgres, GoTrue, PostgREST, Studio, etc.). Local Studio runs at `http://localhost:54323`. The local Postgres runs on port 54322. Use `supabase db reset` to drop and recreate the local database from migrations + seed. Use `supabase status` to see all local service URLs and keys.

links.context: CLI for project setup, local dev, migrations, and deployment
JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for all Supabase services
User authentication — sign-up, sign-in, OAuth, magic links
Query data — select, insert, update, delete, filters, RPC
Row Level Security policies for fine-grained access control
Schema management — create tables, alter, migrate
File uploads — buckets, signed URLs, transformations
pgvector for AI embedding storage and similarity search

related.note: Supabase is built on Postgres — understanding SQL and Postgres features is essential
Next.js is the most common framework used with Supabase via @supabase/ssr
Prisma can connect directly to Supabase Postgres via SUPABASE_DB_URL
Vercel has a native Supabase integration for environment variable injection
Cloudflare D1 + Workers + R2 is an alternative full-stack platform
